fix #19332
HiCache stores alloc() views from free_slots in long-lived nodes, which retains the old host-pool backing storage and causes persistent scheduler RSS growth under hierarchical cache workloads.

lanuch sglang with hicache

sglang.title: [Kernel] Fuse temperature + softmax in sampling for decode speedup
sglang.author: Godmook
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The standard sampling path in sampler.py executes temperature scaling and softmax as two separate CUDA kernels:
logits.div_(sampling_info.temperatures)       # Kernel 1
logits[:] = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)     # Kernel 2
This is called on every decode step and incurs:

2 kernel launc

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add MXFP8 to Marlin GEMM/MoE and refactor Mxfp8LinearOp
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The Marlin kernel already supports FP8 (per-channel/group scales) and MXFP4 (per-32-element e8m0 scales). MXFP8 is a natural combination: FP8 weights (like existing FP8 Marlin) with e8m0 microscaling block scales (like existing MXFP4 Marlin). We just have to wire the kernel building blocks t

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Restrict TRTLLM attention to SM100, fixing GB300 (SM103) hang
vllm.author: stecasta
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
All models hang indefinitely on GB300 (SM103, CC 10.3) during inference with large batch sizes. The GPU shows 99% SM utilization and 0% memory bandwidth. This is a regression introduced by the FlashInfer 0.6.6 to 0.6.7 upgrade, where TRTLLM attention kernels are no longer forward-compatible

vllm.title: [Refactor] Simplify FutureWrapper in MultiprocExecutor
vllm.author: yzong-rh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Refactor FutureWrapper in MultiprocExecutor to simplify the response-fetching pattern.

vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:

FutureWrapper.__init__ now accepts the get_response callable directly and appends itself to the futures queue, instead of the caller managing (future, callable) tu

vllm.title: [Feat][Executor] Introduce RayExecutorV2
vllm.author: jeffreywang88
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Implement RayExecutorV2, a new Ray-based distributed executor that uses MessageQueue (shared memory + TCP fallback) for the control plane instead of Ray compiled graphs. It reuses MultiprocExecutor's MQ-based RPC and NCCL data plane while spawning workers as Ray actors into placement group

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Preserve original ImportError in gRPC server entrypoint
vllm.author: CatherineSue
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix misleading error message when smg-grpc-servicer is installed but has a broken internal import (e.g. version mismatch between servicer and vllm, or a missing transitive dependency like grpcio).
Currently from None suppresses the original traceback, so the user only sees:
ImportError: smg-

vllm.title: [Perf] DSV3.2 Indexer Fused Weights Projection
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fuse the WK and Weights_Proj projections in the DSV3.2 Indexer. This is an alternative optimization to #35968, which overlaps the projections instead of fusing them. Doing the fusion provides a greater speedup:
Benchmark timings for DSV3.2 NVFP4 on 8xB200 (TP8, No Specdec)
BS128 8k/1k
Fused

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Use dedicated MM processor cache in /tokenize to prevent sender-cache pollution
vllm.author: sergey-zinchenko
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix /v1/chat/completions failing after /tokenize is called with the same multimodal input (e.g. an image).
/tokenize runs multimodal preprocessing but does not send anything to the engine core. This leaves stale sender-cache entries that cause subsequent /v1/chat/completions requests to fail

vllm.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Fix ROCm runtime failure due to missing symbol
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow up for #32996
Failed to import from vllm._C with ImportError('/projects/ROCm/vllm_upstream/vllm/_C.abi3.so: undefined symbol: _Z28silu_and_mul_per_block_quantRN2at6TensorERKS0_S1_lSt8optionalIS0_Eb')
The file is not built for ROCm, so the import of torch._C fails

vllm.title: [Kernel] [Helion] Use warning_once in get_gpu_name to prevent log spam
vllm.author: gmagogsfm
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Replace logger.warning() with logger.warning_once() in get_gpu_name() when device_id is None. This prevents repeated warnings in multi-node setups while still alerting users to the potential issue.
